methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $31M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Ares Industries

data.narrative:
CX2 Industries, a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Nathan Mintz and Porter Smith, has raised $31M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—including same-stage defense sector companies Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $31M raised as a value proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage step-ups, implying a $62M valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $300M median, with a range spanning $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence). The peer method commands substantially more weight given the availability of direct comparable transactions in the defense vertical at identical stage.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $53M (low) to $345M (median to high). The median implied value is $181M. The wide dispersion—driven by stage-progression underweighting relative to peer comps—reflects material uncertainty around whether CX2 commands a premium or discount relative to Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries on comparable technical or market position. Resolution of competitive differentiation and customer traction would narrow this range materially.
